subtopics.explanation: This lesson covers For Loop. Select this topic, then open the Patterns dropdown for 30+ examples and interview problems.

Topic: For Loop
Section: week 2
Open Patterns dropdown for 30+ examples!

subtopics.title: Right Triangle Stars
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Print a right-angled triangle of stars. Row i has i stars.
subtopics.explanation: Interview classic. Outer loop = rows, inner loop = stars per row.

subtopics.title: Inverted Right Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Stars decrease each row — common variation in coding tests.
subtopics.explanation: Start inner loop from i down to 1, or use (rows - i + 1) stars.

subtopics.title: Centered Pyramid
subtopics.definition: [Easy] A centered pyramid uses spaces before stars. Row i has (rows-i) spaces and (2*i-1) stars.
subtopics.explanation: Very common in TCS, Infosys, and campus drives. Practice spacing formula.

subtopics.title: Inverted Pyramid
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Upside-down centered pyramid — mirror of the pyramid pattern.
subtopics.explanation: Loop i from rows down to 1. Spaces increase as i decreases.

subtopics.title: Diamond Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Combine upward and downward pyramids to form a diamond.
subtopics.explanation: Interview favorite. Handle middle row once; watch off-by-one on the lower half.

subtopics.title: Hollow Diamond
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Print only the border stars of a diamond — tests condition logic inside loops.
subtopics.explanation: Print star only at edges: j==0, j==2*i-2, or similar boundary checks.

subtopics.title: Number Pyramid (1 2 3)
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Each row prints numbers 1 through i.
subtopics.explanation: Same nested loop structure as star triangle but print j instead of *.

subtopics.title: Same Number Pyramid
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Row i prints the number i repeated i times: 1, 2 2, 3 3 3...
subtopics.explanation: Frequently asked in Wipro and Capgemini pattern rounds.

subtopics.title: Floyd's Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Consecutive numbers across rows: 1; 2 3; 4 5 6...
subtopics.explanation: Use a counter variable incremented in the inner loop.

subtopics.title: Pascal's Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Each number is sum of two numbers above it. Classic interview pattern.
subtopics.explanation: Use combinatorics or build row-by-row with previous row values.

subtopics.title: Full Square of Stars
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Print a solid square of stars with equal rows and columns.
subtopics.explanation: Both loops run 1..n. Simple warm-up before hollow square.

subtopics.title: Hollow Square
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print stars only on the border of a square.
subtopics.explanation: Star when i==1, i==n, j==1, or j==n; otherwise print space.

subtopics.title: Rectangle Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Rectangle with different row and column counts.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop = rows, inner loop = cols. Same logic as square with different limits.

subtopics.title: Right Arrow
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Arrow pointing right using stars — tests asymmetric nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Increase stars then decrease, or use two separate loop blocks.

subtopics.title: Hourglass Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Inverted pyramid followed by upright pyramid — common visual pattern.
subtopics.explanation: Combine two loop blocks with shared middle row optional.

subtopics.title: Butterfly Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Butterfly / wing pattern using stars and spaces — advanced nested loop problem.
subtopics.explanation: Split each row into left wing, gap, right wing with mirror logic.

subtopics.title: Plus / Cross Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print a plus sign shape with stars.
subtopics.explanation: Middle row and middle column are full; others sparse.

subtopics.title: Alphabet Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Row i prints letters A through (A + i - 1).
subtopics.sampleOutput:
A 
A B 
A B C 
A B C D 
A B C D E

subtopics.title: Binary Number Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print binary sequence 1, 10, 11, 100... per row.
subtopics.explanation: Convert row number to binary or build with bit logic.

subtopics.title: Fibonacci Series
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Generate first N Fibonacci numbers using a loop.
subtopics.explanation: Interview must-know. a=0, b=1, then next = a+b, shift values each iteration.

subtopics.title: Factorial of N
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Multiply numbers 1 to N in a loop.
subtopics.explanation: Classic loop accumulation. Watch overflow for large N.

subtopics.title: Prime Numbers in Range
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print all primes between 2 and N using nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop tries each number; inner loop checks divisors up to sqrt(n).

subtopics.title: Armstrong Number Check
subtopics.definition: [Hard] An Armstrong number equals sum of its digits raised to the power of digit count (e.g. 153).
subtopics.explanation: Extract digits with % 10 and n/=10 in a loop. Very common interview question.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 153 is Armstrong? true

subtopics.title: Reverse a Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Build reversed number digit by digit using while loop.
subtopics.explanation: rev = rev * 10 + digit. Asked in almost every Java fresher interview.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Reversed = 54321

subtopics.title: Palindrome Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Check if a number reads the same forwards and backwards.
subtopics.explanation: Reverse the number and compare with original. Also test with strings.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 121 is palindrome? true

subtopics.title: Sum of Digits
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Add all digits of a number using a while loop.
subtopics.explanation: sum += n % 10; n /= 10; until n becomes 0.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Sum of digits = 35

subtopics.title: Count Digits
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Count how many digits a number has.
subtopics.explanation: Divide by 10 repeatedly until number becomes 0.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 987654321 has 9 digits

subtopics.title: Multiplication Table
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Print multiplication table of N using nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop = multiplier 1..10, or nested for full table grid.

subtopics.title: GCD (Euclidean Algorithm)
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Find greatest common divisor using a while loop.
subtopics.explanation: while (b != 0) { temp = b; b = a % b; a = temp; }
subtopics.sampleOutput: GCD = 6

subtopics.title: Find Largest in Array
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Loop through array to find maximum element.
subtopics.explanation: Initialize max = arr[0], compare each element. Same pattern for min and sum.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Max = 9

subtopics.title: Sum of 1 to N
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Accumulate running total from 1 to N — foundation for averages and statistics.
subtopics.explanation: sum = 0; for i 1..n sum += i. Formula n*(n+1)/2 gives same answer.

subtopics.title: Perfect Number Check
subtopics.definition: [Hard] A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (e.g. 6 = 1+2+3).
subtopics.explanation: Loop i from 1 to n/2, add if n % i == 0. Compare sum with n.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 28 is perfect? true

subtopics.title: Power of a Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Compute base^exp using a loop instead of Math.pow.
subtopics.explanation: result = 1; multiply by base exp times. Handle negative exp separately.

subtopics.sampleOutput: 987654321 has 9 digits

subtopics.title: Multiplication Table
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Print multiplication table of N using nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop = multiplier 1..10, or nested for full table grid.

subtopics.title: GCD (Euclidean Algorithm)
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Find greatest common divisor using a while loop.
subtopics.explanation: while (b != 0) { temp = b; b = a % b; a = temp; }
subtopics.sampleOutput: GCD = 6

subtopics.title: Find Largest in Array
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Loop through array to find maximum element.
subtopics.explanation: Initialize max = arr[0], compare each element. Same pattern for min and sum.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Max = 9

subtopics.title: Sum of 1 to N
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Accumulate running total from 1 to N — foundation for averages and statistics.
subtopics.explanation: sum = 0; for i 1..n sum += i. Formula n*(n+1)/2 gives same answer.

subtopics.title: Perfect Number Check
subtopics.definition: [Hard] A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (e.g. 6 = 1+2+3).
subtopics.explanation: Loop i from 1 to n/2, add if n % i == 0. Compare sum with n.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 28 is perfect? true

subtopics.title: Power of a Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Compute base^exp using a loop instead of Math.pow.
subtopics.explanation: result = 1; multiply by base exp times. Handle negative exp separately.

subtopics.title: Nested Loops
subtopics.definition: Nested Loops — learn the concept in plain language, then study the code and click Run.
subtopics.explanation: This lesson covers Nested Loops. Select this topic, then open the Patterns dropdown for 30+ examples and interview problems.
subtopics.sampleOutput:
Topic: Nested Loops
Section: week 2
Open Patterns dropdown for 30+ examples!

subtopics.title: Right Triangle Stars
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Print a right-angled triangle of stars. Row i has i stars.
subtopics.explanation: Interview classic. Outer loop = rows, inner loop = stars per row.

subtopics.title: Inverted Right Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Stars decrease each row — common variation in coding tests.
subtopics.explanation: Start inner loop from i down to 1, or use (rows - i + 1) stars.

subtopics.title: Centered Pyramid
subtopics.definition: [Easy] A centered pyramid uses spaces before stars. Row i has (rows-i) spaces and (2*i-1) stars.
subtopics.explanation: Very common in TCS, Infosys, and campus drives. Practice spacing formula.

subtopics.title: Inverted Pyramid
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Upside-down centered pyramid — mirror of the pyramid pattern.
subtopics.explanation: Loop i from rows down to 1. Spaces increase as i decreases.

subtopics.title: Diamond Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Combine upward and downward pyramids to form a diamond.
subtopics.explanation: Interview favorite. Handle middle row once; watch off-by-one on the lower half.

subtopics.title: Hollow Diamond
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Print only the border stars of a diamond — tests condition logic inside loops.
subtopics.explanation: Print star only at edges: j==0, j==2*i-2, or similar boundary checks.

subtopics.title: Number Pyramid (1 2 3)
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Each row prints numbers 1 through i.
subtopics.explanation: Same nested loop structure as star triangle but print j instead of *.

subtopics.title: Same Number Pyramid
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Row i prints the number i repeated i times: 1, 2 2, 3 3 3...
subtopics.explanation: Frequently asked in Wipro and Capgemini pattern rounds.

subtopics.title: Floyd's Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Consecutive numbers across rows: 1; 2 3; 4 5 6...
subtopics.explanation: Use a counter variable incremented in the inner loop.

subtopics.title: Pascal's Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Each number is sum of two numbers above it. Classic interview pattern.
subtopics.explanation: Use combinatorics or build row-by-row with previous row values.

subtopics.title: Full Square of Stars
subtopics.definition: [Easy] Print a solid square of stars with equal rows and columns.
subtopics.explanation: Both loops run 1..n. Simple warm-up before hollow square.

subtopics.title: Hollow Square
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print stars only on the border of a square.
subtopics.explanation: Star when i==1, i==n, j==1, or j==n; otherwise print space.

subtopics.title: Rectangle Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Rectangle with different row and column counts.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop = rows, inner loop = cols. Same logic as square with different limits.

subtopics.title: Right Arrow
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Arrow pointing right using stars — tests asymmetric nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Increase stars then decrease, or use two separate loop blocks.

subtopics.title: Hourglass Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Inverted pyramid followed by upright pyramid — common visual pattern.
subtopics.explanation: Combine two loop blocks with shared middle row optional.

subtopics.title: Butterfly Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Butterfly / wing pattern using stars and spaces — advanced nested loop problem.
subtopics.explanation: Split each row into left wing, gap, right wing with mirror logic.

subtopics.title: Plus / Cross Pattern
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print a plus sign shape with stars.
subtopics.explanation: Middle row and middle column are full; others sparse.

subtopics.title: Alphabet Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Row i prints letters A through (A + i - 1).
subtopics.sampleOutput:
A 
A B 
A B C 
A B C D 
A B C D E

subtopics.title: Binary Number Triangle
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print binary sequence 1, 10, 11, 100... per row.
subtopics.explanation: Convert row number to binary or build with bit logic.

subtopics.title: Fibonacci Series
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Generate first N Fibonacci numbers using a loop.
subtopics.explanation: Interview must-know. a=0, b=1, then next = a+b, shift values each iteration.

subtopics.title: Factorial of N
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Multiply numbers 1 to N in a loop.
subtopics.explanation: Classic loop accumulation. Watch overflow for large N.

subtopics.title: Prime Numbers in Range
subtopics.definition: [Medium] Print all primes between 2 and N using nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop tries each number; inner loop checks divisors up to sqrt(n).

subtopics.title: Armstrong Number Check
subtopics.definition: [Hard] An Armstrong number equals sum of its digits raised to the power of digit count (e.g. 153).
subtopics.explanation: Extract digits with % 10 and n/=10 in a loop. Very common interview question.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 153 is Armstrong? true

subtopics.title: Reverse a Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Build reversed number digit by digit using while loop.
subtopics.explanation: rev = rev * 10 + digit. Asked in almost every Java fresher interview.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Reversed = 54321

subtopics.title: Palindrome Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Check if a number reads the same forwards and backwards.
subtopics.explanation: Reverse the number and compare with original. Also test with strings.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 121 is palindrome? true

subtopics.title: Sum of Digits
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Add all digits of a number using a while loop.
subtopics.explanation: sum += n % 10; n /= 10; until n becomes 0.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Sum of digits = 35

subtopics.title: Count Digits
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Count how many digits a number has.
subtopics.explanation: Divide by 10 repeatedly until number becomes 0.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 987654321 has 9 digits

subtopics.title: Multiplication Table
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Print multiplication table of N using nested loops.
subtopics.explanation: Outer loop = multiplier 1..10, or nested for full table grid.

subtopics.title: GCD (Euclidean Algorithm)
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Find greatest common divisor using a while loop.
subtopics.explanation: while (b != 0) { temp = b; b = a % b; a = temp; }
subtopics.sampleOutput: GCD = 6

subtopics.title: Find Largest in Array
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Loop through array to find maximum element.
subtopics.explanation: Initialize max = arr[0], compare each element. Same pattern for min and sum.
subtopics.sampleOutput: Max = 9

subtopics.title: Sum of 1 to N
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Accumulate running total from 1 to N — foundation for averages and statistics.
subtopics.explanation: sum = 0; for i 1..n sum += i. Formula n*(n+1)/2 gives same answer.

subtopics.title: Perfect Number Check
subtopics.definition: [Hard] A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (e.g. 6 = 1+2+3).
subtopics.explanation: Loop i from 1 to n/2, add if n % i == 0. Compare sum with n.
subtopics.sampleOutput: 28 is perfect? true

subtopics.title: Power of a Number
subtopics.definition: [Hard] Compute base^exp using a loop instead of Math.pow.
subtopics.explanation: result = 1; multiply by base exp times. Handle negative exp separately.
